titleStmt.title: Letter from M.L. Waller to Sir Robert Chalmers, 5 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M.L. Waller

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter from M.L. Waller of the Home Office in Whitehall, London, to Sir Robert Chalmers. Waller is writing to Chalmers about a list of German civilian Prisoners of War, previously resident in Ireland, whom it is proposed to repatriate in exchange for British subjects of the same class being detained in Germany. Enclosed with the letter is a list of names, age, addresses and occupations of German civilians resident in Ireland.
Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5618/11613

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to transmit to you
herewith, for the information of the Lords Justices, a list
of German Civilian Prisoners of War, previously resident
in Ireland, whom it is proposed to repatriate at an early date, in
exchange for British subjects of the same class, at present
detained in Germany. I am accordingly to request you to be so good as to furnish
a report in due course, stating whether there is, in your opinion,
any objection to the repatriation of any of the persons named
on the list. I am,
Sir
your obedient Servant, M.L. Waller The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle. Immediate
I Reply
II C.C. D.M.P.
for report, please
EOF
7. VII. 16. 1. Send minutes to I'r Command
regarding case in R.S.C. area
2. Send C.C. DMP file
W R OConnell
8.7.16 1. Done
Number. Name. Nat. Age Occupation. Place of Arrest.
138. ARLD, Paul. Ger. 55. Butcher. Dublin.
19. EHRICH, Max " 26. Confectioner. Cork.
605. FLANNER, John " 50. Butcher. Dublin.
609. FERLl, Richard " 28. Watchmaker. "
659. HARTMANN, Andrew. " 47. Cook. "
132. HEIL, H. " 29. Photographer. "
640. HUSTER, E.O. " 52. Foreman. Belfast.
7. LAMBERT, J. " 39. Waiter. Enniskellin.
281. SPREKER, E. " 35. Butcher. Queenstown.
582. TAUBERSCHMIDT " 50. Baker. Belfast.
657. WIELAND, Carl. " 52. Butcher. Bray.
103. WORSCHEG, Pail. " 28 Waiter. Dublin.
